question_text: Prove that the lengths of the tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are equal.
solution_text:
Consider a circle centered at O. 
Let PR and QR are tangents drawn from an external point R to the circle touching at points P and Q 
respectively. 
Join OR. 
 
Proof: 
In ∆OPR and ∆OQR, 
OP = OQ  
... (Radii of the same circle) 
∠OPR = ∠OQR  
…. (Since PR and QR are tangents to the circle) 
OR = OR  
... (Common side) 
∴ ∆OPR ≅ ∆OQ R  
….(By R.H.S) 
∴ PR = QR  
….(c.p.c.t) 
Thus, tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are equal. 
Marks: 4
subject: maths

question_text:
Draw a circle of radius 4 cm. Draw two tangents to the circle inclined at an angle of 60° to 
each other.
solution_text:
Steps of construction: 
(i) Take a point O on the plane of the paper and draw a circle of radius OA = 4 cm. 
(ii) Produce OA to B such that OA = AB = 4 cm. 
(iii) Draw a circle with centre at A and radius AB. 
(iv) Suppose it cuts the circle drawn in step (i) at P and Q. 
(v) Join BP and BQ to get the desired tangents. 
 
 
                        www.vedantu.com 
 
 14 / 19 
 
 
Justification: 
In ∆OAP, OA = OP = 4 cm   
... (radii of the same circle) 
Also, AP = 4 cm  
….(Radius of the circle with centre A) 
∴ ∆OAP is equilateral. 
∴ ∠PAO = 60° 
∴ ∠BAP = 120° 
In ∆BAP, we have BA = AP and ∠ BAP = 120° 
∴ ∠ABP = ∠APB = 30° 
Similarly we can get ∠ABQ = 30° 
∴ ∠PBQ = 60° 
Marks: 4

question_text:
The houses in a row numbered consecutively from 1 to 49. Show that there exists a value of X 
such that sum of numbers of houses preceding the house numbered X is equal to sum of the numbers of 
houses following X.
solution_text:
Let there be a value of x such that the sum of the numbers of the houses preceding the house 
numbered x is equal to the sum of the numbers of the houses following it. 
That is, 1 + 2 + 3 + . . . . + (x – 1) = (x + 1) + (x + 2) + . . . . . + 49 
∴ 1 + 2 + 3 + . . . . + (x – 1) 
= [1 + 2 + .... + x + (x – 1) + .... + 49] – (1 + 2 + 3 +. . . . + x)

∴ x(x – 1) + x(1 + x) = 49 × 50 
∴ x2 – x + x + x2 = 49 × 50 
∴ x2 = 49 × 50 
∴ x2 = 49 × 25 
∴ x = 7 × 5 = 35 
Since x is not a fraction, the value of x satisfying the given condition exists and is equal to 35.

question_text: What is the common difference of an A.P. in which a21 - a7 = 84 ?
solution_text:
Given 
a21 – a7 =84 ....................(1) 
In an A.P a1, a2, a3, a4 ………… 
an = a1 + (n – 1)d  
d = common difference 
a21 = a1 + 20 d ……………..(2) 
a7 = a1 + 6d 
……………….(3) 
substituting (2) & (3) in (1) 
a1 + 20d – a1 – 6d = 84 
14d = 84 
d = 6 
 common difference = 6

question_text:
In Figure-5, a quadrilateral ABCD is drawn to circumscribe a circle. 
Prove that AB+CD=BC+AD.                                                              2 Marks                        
 
Ans: As we know, length of tangents drawn from an external point are equal. 
Therefore, we can write 
⇒AP=AS ---(1) 
⇒BP=BQ ---(2) 
⇒CR=CQ ---(3) 
⇒BR=DS ---(4) 
On adding equation (1), (2), (3) and (4), we get 
⇒AP+BP+CR+DR=AS+BQ+CQ+DS 
⇒ (AP+BP)+(CR+DR)=(AS+DS)+(BQ+CQ) 
⇒AB+CD=AD+BC 
Hence, proved that AB+CD=AD+BC

=2Ap 
=2×12 
=24 
Therefore, perimeter of Δ ABC is 24 cm.

The present age of a father is three years more than three times the age of 
his son. Three years hence the father’s age will be 10 years more than twice 
the age of the son. Determine their present ages.                               3 Marks 
Ans: Let the son’s age be x. 
Given, age of the father is three years more than three times the age of his son. 
Therefore, father’s age is 3x+3. 
After three years, 
Age of the son =x+3 
Age of the father =3x+3+3 ---(1) 
But, according to question, after three years the father’s age will be 10 years 
more than twice the age of the son. 
Age of the father 10+2(x+3) ---(2) 
From (1) and (2), we get 
⇒3x+6=10+2(x+3) 
⇒3x+6=10+2x+6 
⇒x=10 
So, the present age of son is 10 years. 
Present age of father =3×10+3 
=33 years 
Therefore, the present age of son is 10 years and father is 33 years.

question_text:
Using the empirical relationship between the three measures of central tendency, find 
the median of a distribution, whose mean is 169 and mode is 175. 
 
 
   2 
Ans: The empirical relationship between mean, median, and mode is given by: 
Mean – Mode = 3(Mean−Median) 
Given, Mean = 169 and Mode = 175: 
169 – 175 = 3(169 − Median) 
−6 = 3(169 − Median) 
−6 = 507 − 3Median 
3Median = 507 + 6 
3Median = 513 
Median = 513
3  = 171 
 
 
 
 
www.vedantu.com   
4  
 
  
Therefore, the median is 171.

question_text:
Two concentric circles are of radii 4 cm and 3 cm . Find the length of the chord of the 
larger circle which touches the smaller circle. 
 
 
 
 
 
   2 
Ans:  
 
To find the length of the chord of the larger circle that touches the smaller circle. 
You have two concentric circles, meaning they share the same center. The radius of the larger 
circle is 4 cm, and the radius of the smaller circle is 3 cm. A chord of the larger circle touches 
the smaller circle. 
Let O be the center of both circles. Since the chord touches the smaller circle, it is tangent to 
it. Let's call the chord AB and the point where the chord touches the smaller circle P. 
The radius drawn to a tangent at the point of tangency is perpendicular to the tangent. 
Therefore, OP (the radius of the smaller circle) is perpendicular to the chord AB. 
Since O s the center, OP is the radius of the smaller circle (3 cm), and OA is the radius of the 
larger circle (4 cm). Let M be the midpoint of the chord AB.

question_text:
There are 80 cards numbered from 1 to 80. One card is drawn at random from them. 
Find the probability that the number on the selected card is not divisible by 8.  
   2 
Ans: There are 80 cards, so the total number of possible outcomes is 80. 
80
8  = 10 
So, there are 10 cards with numbers divisible by 8 (8, 16, 24, 32, 40, 48, 56, 64, 72, 80). 
Subtract the number of cards divisible by 8 from the total number of cards: 
80 - 10 = 70 
So, there are 70 cards that are not divisible by 8. 
The probability is the number of favorable outcomes (cards not divisible by 8) divided by the 
total number of outcomes (total cards): 
Probability = Number of cards not divisible by 8
Total number of cards
= 70
